operation: First, a new 2D CadQuery sketch is initialized. Four coordinate points (2, 2), (5, 5), (8, 8), and (10, 10), all located on the y=x diagonal line, are pushed as active sketch origins. Three sets of centered, axis-aligned rectangles are drawn at each of the four active origins: first 1-unit by 1-unit rectangles, then 2-unit by 2-unit rectangles, then 3-unit by 3-unit rectangles. Overlapping rectangles at each origin point are merged automatically to form a single solid shape per origin, resulting in four distinct shapes aligned along the diagonal.
geometry: This 2D model is composed of four progressively larger square shapes arranged sequentially along a 45-degree diagonal line. Each square is axis-aligned so their corners lie on the alignment diagonal, with each adjacent pair of squares touching exactly at one shared corner point. The smallest square sits at the top end of the diagonal, while the largest is positioned at the bottom end.
function: This 2D pattern is designed as a test artifact for validating 2D sketching workflows and STL export functionality, or can be used as a simple decorative graphical element or calibration marker for manufacturing processes.
